creator: amdmi3@FreeBSD.org
text:
While formating man pages, groff does replace all `-' (U+002D HYPHEN-MINUS) with `ГўВҲВ’' (U+2212 MINUS SIGN). This is never correct as command line argument descriptions, mathematical operator descriptions and other stuff become broken and useless for copypasting and search.

The fix attached is a workaround (thanks swell.k at gmail dot com), but that's not enought as there are other cases of symbol corruption.

Examples from `man sh`:

set -E
set ГўВҖВҗE

<&    >&    <<-   >|
<&    >&    <<ГўВҖВҗ   >|

backquote (``')
backquote (ГўВҖВҳГўВҖВҳГўВҖВҷ) (U+2018 LEFT SINGLE QUOTATION MARK)

I guess the correct fix would be to always use -Tascii for man formatting.

How-To-Repeat: man anything with UTF-8 locale
